question: Why do the Gospels treat Jesus walking on the sea as more than a ‘cool miracle’—what OT ‘divine prerogative’ pattern is being echoed?

choices.A: OT portrays God as one who treads upon the waves; Jesus’ action echoes that divine imagery, implying more than prophetic power.
choices.B: It only shows Jesus found shallow water.
choices.C: It is a parable and not presented as a real event.
choices.D: It proves the disciples invented miracles.

teaching.title: Treading the sea—God’s role echoed
teaching.body: Job and the Psalms use imagery of God walking on the sea and making a path through waters. Mark/Matthew/John portray Jesus doing what the OT reserves as divine. The narrative wants you to connect dots: Jesus is not merely ‘powered up,’ he’s acting in God’s script.
